chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. al-Hasan al-Saffar said: It was narrated to me by Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Hasan B. Zayd B. Ali B. al-Husayn, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim. Indeed, Allah loves those who seek knowledge.'
englishText:
1. al-Hasan B. Zayd B. Ali B. al-Husayn, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim. Indeed, Allah loves those who seek knowledge.'
arabicText: 1- مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ أَلَا وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ بُغَاةَ الْعِلْمِ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. al-Hasan al-Saffar said: It was narrated to me by Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Hasan B. Zayd B. Ali B. al-Husayn, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
al-Hasan B. Zayd B. Ali B. al-Husayn, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim. Indeed, Allah loves those who seek knowledge.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. Hassan, from Muhammad B. Ali, from Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation under all circumstances.'
englishText:
2. Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation under all circumstances.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْعُمَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. Hassan, from Muhammad B. Ali, from Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation under all circumstances.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Yaqoub B. Yazeed, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from a man from our companions, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.'
englishText:
3. Ibn Abi Umayr, from a man from our companions, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from Ameer al-Mu'mineen (Imam Ali) {{AS}}, who said: Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.'
arabicText: 3- يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ.
manual_chain: Yaqoub B. Yazeed, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from a man from our companions, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Ibn Abi Umayr, from a man from our companions, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from Ameer al-Mu'mineen (Imam Ali) {{AS}}, who said: Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation upon every Muslim.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. Hassan, from Muhammad B. Ali, from Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation from the obligations of Allah.'
englishText:
4. Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation from the obligations of Allah.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْعُمَرِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ مِنْ فَرَائِضِ اللَّهِ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. Hassan, from Muhammad B. Ali, from Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Isa B. Abdullah al-Umari narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation from the obligations of Allah.'

chapter: Chapter 1: Regarding the knowledge that seeking it is an obligation upon the people
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. al-Husayn, from Muhammad B. Abdullah, from Isa B. Abdullah, from Ahmad B. Umar B. Ali B. Abi Talib.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Seeking knowledge is an obligation from the obligations of Allah.'
englishText:
5. Ahmad B. Umar B. Ali B. Abi Talib is reported to have said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation from the obligations of Allah.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ عِيسَى بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ع قَالَ طَلَبُ الْعِلْمِ فَرِيضَةٌ مِنْ فَرَائِضِ اللَّهِ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. al-Husayn, from Muhammad B. Abdullah, from Isa B. Abdullah, from Ahmad B. Umar B. Ali B. Abi Talib.
manual_body:
Ahmad B. Umar B. Ali B. Abi Talib is reported to have said:

'Seeking knowledge is an obligation from the obligations of Allah.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman B. Abi Najran and Muhammad B. al-Husayn, from Amro B. Aasim, from al-Mufaddal B. Salih, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Indeed, the teacher of good — there seek forgiveness for him the animals of the earth, the fish of the sea, every living creature in the air, and all the inhabitants of the heaven and the earth. The scholar and the student are equal in reward; the two of them will come on the Day of Resurrection like two racehorses, neck and neck.'
englishText:
1. Jabir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Indeed, the teacher of good — there seek forgiveness for him the animals of the earth, the fish of the sea, every living creature in the air, and all the inhabitants of the heaven and the earth. The scholar and the student are equal in reward; the two of them will come on the Day of Resurrection like two racehorses, neck and neck.'
arabicText: 1- قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي نَجْرَانَ وَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عَاصِمٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِنَّ مُعَلِّمَ الْخَيْرِ يَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ دَوَابُّ الْأَرْضِ وَ حِيتَانُ الْبَحْرِ وَ كُلُّ ذِي رُوحٍ فِي الْهَوَاءِ وَ جَمِيعُ أَهْلِ السَّمَاءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ وَ إِنَّ الْعَالِمَ وَ الْمُتَعَلِّمَ فِي الْأَجْرِ سَوَاءٌ يَأْتِيَانِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كَفَرَسَيْ رِهَانٍ يَزْدَحِمَانِ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Abdul Rahman B. Abi Najran and Muhammad B. al-Husayn, from Amro B. Aasim, from al-Mufaddal B. Salih, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Jabir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Indeed, the teacher of good — there seek forgiveness for him the animals of the earth, the fish of the sea, every living creature in the air, and all the inhabitants of the heaven and the earth. The scholar and the student are equal in reward; the two of them will come on the Day of Resurrection like two racehorses, neck and neck.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Hammad B. Isa, from Abdullah B. Maymoun al-Qaddah, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Whoever travels a path seeking knowledge along it, Allah, the Exalted, will cause him to travel by it a path to Paradise. The angels lower their wings for the seeker of knowledge out of pleasure with him, and those in the heavens and those in the earth seek forgiveness for him — even the fish in the sea. The merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the full moon over the rest of the stars on the night it is full; and the scholars are the heirs of the Prophets {{AS}}. The Prophets {{AS}} bequeath neither dinar nor dirham; rather, they bequeath knowledge.'
englishText:
2. Abdullah B. Maymoun al-Qaddah narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, who said: Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Whoever travels a path seeking knowledge along it, Allah, the Exalted, will cause him to travel by it a path to Paradise. The angels lower their wings for the seeker of knowledge out of pleasure with him, and those in the heavens and those in the earth seek forgiveness for him — even the fish in the sea. The merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the full moon over the rest of the stars on the night it is full; and the scholars are the heirs of the Prophets {{AS}}. The Prophets {{AS}} bequeath neither dinar nor dirham; rather, they bequeath knowledge.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ الْقَدَّاحِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ مَنْ سَلَكَ طَرِيقاً يَطْلُبُ فِيهِ عِلْماً سَلَكَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى بِهِ طَرِيقاً إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ لَتَضَعُ أَجْنِحَتَهَا لِطَالِبِ الْعِلْمِ رِضاً بِهِ وَ إِنَّهُ لَيَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَ مَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ حَتَّى الْحُوتُ فِي الْبَحْرِ وَ فَضْلُ الْعَالِمِ عَلَى الْعَابِدِ كَفَضْلِ الْقَمَرِ لَيْلَةَ الْبَدْرِ عَلَى سَائِرِ الْكَوَاكِبِ وَ إِنَّ الْعُلَمَاءَ وَرَثَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ إِنَّ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ لَمْ يُوَرِّثُوا دِينَاراً وَ لَا دِرْهَماً وَ لَكِنْ وَرَّثُوا الْعِلْمَ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Hammad B. Isa, from Abdullah B. Maymoun al-Qaddah, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abdullah B. Maymoun al-Qaddah narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, who said: Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'Whoever travels a path seeking knowledge along it, Allah, the Exalted, will cause him to travel by it a path to Paradise. The angels lower their wings for the seeker of knowledge out of pleasure with him, and those in the heavens and those in the earth seek forgiveness for him — even the fish in the sea. The merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the full moon over the rest of the stars on the night it is full; and the scholars are the heirs of the Prophets {{AS}}. The Prophets {{AS}} bequeath neither dinar nor dirham; rather, they bequeath knowledge.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Abdul Rahman B. al-Hajjaj, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The seeker of knowledge — everything seeks forgiveness for him, even the fish in the seas and the birds in the expanse of the sky.'
englishText:
3. Abdul Rahman B. al-Hajjaj narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'The seeker of knowledge — everything seeks forgiveness for him, even the fish in the seas and the birds in the expanse of the sky.'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ إِنَّ طَالِبَ الْعِلْمِ يَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ حَتَّى الْحِيتَانُ فِي الْبِحَارِ وَ الطَّيْرُ فِي جَوِّ السَّمَاءِ.
manual_chain: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Abdul Rahman B. al-Hajjaj, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abdul Rahman B. al-Hajjaj narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'The seeker of knowledge — everything seeks forgiveness for him, even the fish in the seas and the birds in the expanse of the sky.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: al-Hasan B. Ali, from al-Abbas B. Aamir, from Fuzeyl B. Usman, from Abu Ubeyda, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'All the animals of the earth send blessings upon the seeker of knowledge, even the fish in the sea.'
englishText:
4. Abu Ubeyda narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'All the animals of the earth send blessings upon the seeker of knowledge, even the fish in the sea.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ عَامِرٍ عَنْ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ إِنَّ جَمِيعَ دَوَابِّ الْأَرْضِ لَتُصَلِّي عَلَى طَالِبِ الْعِلْمِ حَتَّى الْحِيتَانُ فِي الْبَحْرِ.
manual_chain: al-Hasan B. Ali, from al-Abbas B. Aamir, from Fuzeyl B. Usman, from Abu Ubeyda, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abu Ubeyda narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'All the animals of the earth send blessings upon the seeker of knowledge, even the fish in the sea.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Husayn B. Sayf, from his father, from Amro B. Shimr, who said: Jabir narrated to me, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The teacher of good — there seek forgiveness for him the animals of the earth and the fish of the sea, and everything small and great in the earth of Allah and His heaven.'
englishText:
5. Jabir narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'The teacher of good — there seek forgiveness for him the animals of the earth and the fish of the sea, and everything small and great in the earth of Allah and His heaven.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَيْفٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي جَابِرٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ مُعَلِّمُ الْخَيْرِ يَسْتَغْفِرُ لَهُ دَوَابُّ الْأَرْضِ وَ حِيتَانُ الْبَحْرِ وَ كُلُّ صَغِيرَةٍ وَ كَبِيرَةٍ فِي أَرْضِ اللَّهِ وَ سَمَائِهِ.
manual_chain: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Husayn B. Sayf, from his father, from Amro B. Shimr, who said: Jabir narrated to me, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Jabir narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'The teacher of good — there seek forgiveness for him the animals of the earth and the fish of the sea, and everything small and great in the earth of Allah and His heaven.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Husayn B. Sayf, from his father, from Wahab B. Saeed, from al-Husayn B. al-Sabbah al-Nakha'i, who said: Jareer B. Abdullah al-Bajali narrated to me, from the Prophet {{SAW}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah revealed to me: Whoever travels a way seeking knowledge along it, I shall ease for him a path to Paradise.'
englishText:
6. Jareer B. Abdullah al-Bajali narrates that the Prophet {{SAW}} said:

'Allah revealed to me: Whoever travels a way seeking knowledge along it, I shall ease for him a path to Paradise.'
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَيْفٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ وَهَبِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الصَّبَّاحِ النَّخَعِيِّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي جَرِيرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَجَلِيُّ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ص قَالَ أَوْحَى اللَّهُ إِلَيَّ مَنْ سَلَكَ طَرِيقاً يَطْلُبُ فِيهِ الْعِلْمَ سَهَّلْتُ لَهُ طَرِيقاً إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ.
manual_chain: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Husayn B. Sayf, from his father, from Wahab B. Saeed, from al-Husayn B. al-Sabbah al-Nakha'i, who said: Jareer B. Abdullah al-Bajali narrated to me, from the Prophet {{SAW}}.
manual_body:
Jareer B. Abdullah al-Bajali narrates that the Prophet {{SAW}} said:

'Allah revealed to me: Whoever travels a way seeking knowledge along it, I shall ease for him a path to Paradise.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Husayn B. Sayf, from his father, from Suleyman B. Amro al-Nakha'i, from Abdullah B. al-Hasan B. al-Hasan B. Ali, from his father, from Imam Ali {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The seeker of knowledge is escorted by a thousand angels from the various heavens, saying: O Lord, send blessings upon Muhammad {{SAW}} and the Progeny of Muhammad {{AS}}.'
englishText:
7. Abdullah B. al-Hasan B. al-Hasan B. Ali, from his father, from Imam Ali {{AS}}, who said:

'The seeker of knowledge is escorted by a thousand angels from the various heavens, saying: O Lord, send blessings upon Muhammad {{SAW}} and the Progeny of Muhammad {{AS}}.'

manual_chain: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from al-Husayn B. Sayf, from his father, from Suleyman B. Amro al-Nakha'i, from Abdullah B. al-Hasan B. al-Hasan B. Ali, from his father, from Imam Ali {{AS}}.

Abdullah B. al-Hasan B. al-Hasan B. Ali, from his father, from Imam Ali {{AS}}, who said:

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Hasan B. Mahboub, from Amro B. Abi al-Miqdam, from Jabir B. Yazeed al-Ju'fi, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The teacher and the student are both partners in the reward. For the teacher there are two rewards, and for the student one reward; and there is no good in anything besides that.'
englishText:
8. Jabir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'The teacher and the student are both partners in the reward. For the teacher there are two rewards, and for the student one reward; and there is no good in anything besides that.'
arabicText: 8- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ أَبِي الْمِقْدَامِ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ الْجُعْفِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ الْمُعَلِّمُ وَ الْمُتَعَلِّمُ شَرِيكَانِ فِي الْأَجْرِ لِلْمُعَلِّمِ أَجْرَانِ وَ لِلْمُتَعَلِّمِ أَجْرٌ وَ لَا خَيْرَ فِي سِوَى ذَلِكَ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Hasan B. Mahboub, from Amro B. Abi al-Miqdam, from Jabir B. Yazeed al-Ju'fi, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Jabir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'The teacher and the student are both partners in the reward. For the teacher there are two rewards, and for the student one reward; and there is no good in anything besides that.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. al-Husayn B. Amro B. Usman and al-Hasan B. Ali B. Fazzal, together from Jameel B. Darraj, from Muhammad B. Muslim, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Whoever among you learns knowledge will have a reward like the reward of the one who taught it, and the teacher has a merit over him. So learn knowledge from the bearers of knowledge, and teach it to your brethren, just as the scholars taught you.'

9. Muhammad B. Muslim narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Whoever among you learns knowledge will have a reward like the reward of the one who taught it, and the teacher has a merit over him. So learn knowledge from the bearers of knowledge, and teach it to your brethren, just as the scholars taught you.'
arabicText: 9- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ عُثْمَانَ وَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ جَمِيعاً عَنْ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ مَنْ تَعَلَّمَ مِنْكُمُ الْعِلْمَ كَانَ لَهُ مِنَ الْأَجْرِ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ الَّذِي عَلَّمَهُ وَ لَهُ الْفَضْلُ عَلَيْهِ فَتَعَلَّمُوا الْعِلْمَ مِنْ حَمَلَةِ الْعِلْمِ وَ عَلِّمُوهُ إِخْوَانَكُمْ كَمَا عَلَّمَكُمُ الْعُلَمَاءُ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. al-Husayn B. Amro B. Usman and al-Hasan B. Ali B. Fazzal, together from Jameel B. Darraj, from Muhammad B. Muslim, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.

Muhammad B. Muslim narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student

'The believing scholar is of greater reward than the one who fasts, the one who stands in prayer, and the one who fights in the way of Allah. When he dies, a breach is opened in Islam that nothing can fill until the Day of Resurrection.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Ali B. al-Hakam, from Abu Hamza, from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} saying.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Whoever teaches some good will have a reward like the reward of the one who acts upon it.' I said: 'And if he teaches it to someone else, will that flow to him?' He {{AS}} said: 'Even if he teaches it to all the people, it flows to him.' I said: 'And if he has died?' He {{AS}} said: 'Even if he has died.'

'Whoever teaches some good will have a reward like the reward of the one who acts upon it.' I said: 'And if he teaches it to someone else, will that flow to him?' He {{AS}} said: 'Even if he teaches it to all the people, it flows to him.' I said: 'And if he has died?' He {{AS}} said: 'Even if he has died.'

thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Ali B. al-Hakam, from Fuzeyl B. Usman, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.

thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad, from al-Barqy, from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Ali B. Yaqteen, from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} saying.

'Whoever teaches some good will have his reward.' I said: 'And if he teaches it to someone else?' He {{AS}} said: 'It flows to him, even if he teaches it to all the people.' And one of them added in it: I said: 'And if he has died?' He {{AS}} said: 'Even if he has died.'

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Muhammad B. Ali, from al-Husayn B. Ali B. Yusuf, from Muqatil B. Muqatil, from al-Rabie B. Muhammad al-Muslimy, from Jabir, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'There is no servant who goes out morning and evening seeking knowledge but that he plunges into mercy, immersed in it.'
englishText:
14. Jabir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'There is no servant who goes out morning and evening seeking knowledge but that he plunges into mercy, immersed in it.'
arabicText: 14- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ يُوسُفَ عَنْ مُقَاتِلِ بْنِ مُقَاتِلٍ عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْمُسْلِمِيِّ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ يَغْدُو فِي طَلَبِ الْعِلْمِ وَ يَرُوحُ إِلَّا خَاضَ مِنَ الرَّحْمَةِ خَوْضاً.

thaqalaynMatn: 'The teacher and the student are equal in reward.'

15. Suleyman al-Ja'fari, from a man, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who said:

'The teacher and the student are equal in reward.'
arabicText: 15- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ الْجَعْفَرِيِّ عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ الْمُعَلِّمُ وَ الْمُتَعَلِّمُ فِي الْأَجْرِ سَوَاءٌ.

Suleyman al-Ja'fari, from a man, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who said:

chapter: Chapter 2: Rewards of the Teacher and the Student
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Abdullah B. Muhammad, from Muhammad B. al-Husayn, from Muhammad B. al-Hammad al-Harisy, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'A man will come on the Day of Resurrection with good deeds like heaped clouds or like towering mountains. He will say: O Lord, from where is this for me, when I did not do these? Allah will say: This is your knowledge which you taught to the people; they acted upon it after you.'
englishText:
16. Muhammad B. al-Hammad al-Harisy, from his father, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who narrates that Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'A man will come on the Day of Resurrection with good deeds like heaped clouds or like towering mountains. He will say: O Lord, from where is this for me, when I did not do these? Allah will say: This is your knowledge which you taught to the people; they acted upon it after you.'
arabicText: 16- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَمَّادِ الْحَارِثِيِّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ يَجِيءُ الرَّجُلُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَ لَهُ مِنَ الْحَسَنَاتِ كَالسَّحَابِ الرُّكَامِ أَوْ كَالْجِبَالِ الرَّوَاسِي فَيَقُولُ يَا رَبِّ أَنَّى لِي هَذَا وَ لَمْ أَعْمَلْهَا فَيَقُولُ هَذَا عِلْمُكَ الَّذِي عَلَّمْتَهُ النَّاسَ يُعْمَلُ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِكَ.

chapter: Chapter 3: Recognition of the Scholar — whoever recognises him recognises Allah, and whoever denies him denies Allah the Exalted; and the cause by which one is granted recognition of him
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Muhammad B. al-Husayn B. Sagheer, from the one who narrated to him, from Rabie B. Abdullah, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah has refused to let things proceed except through their means. So He appointed for everything a means, and for every means an explanation, and for every explanation a knowledge, and for every knowledge a speaking gate. Whoever recognises it has recognised it, and whoever is ignorant of it is ignorant of it. That is the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, and us.'
englishText:
1. Rabie B. Abdullah narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Allah has refused to let things proceed except through their means. So He appointed for everything a means, and for every means an explanation, and for every explanation a knowledge, and for every knowledge a speaking gate. Whoever recognises it has recognised it, and whoever is ignorant of it is ignorant of it. That is the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, and us.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ صَغِيرٍ عَمَّنْ حَدَّثَهُ عَنْ رَبِيعِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَنَّهُ قَالَ أَبَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يُجْرِيَ الْأَشْيَاءَ إِلَّا بِالْأَسْبَابِ فَجَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ سَبَباً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ سَبَبٍ شَرْحاً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَرْحٍ عِلْماً وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ عِلْمٍ بَاباً نَاطِقاً عَرَفَهُ مَنْ عَرَفَهُ وَ جَهِلَهُ مَنْ جَهِلَهُ ذَلِكَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص وَ نَحْنُ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Muhammad B. al-Husayn B. Sagheer, from the one who narrated to him, from Rabie B. Abdullah, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Rabie B. Abdullah narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

So He appointed for everything a means, and for every means an explanation, and for every explanation a knowledge, and for every knowledge a speaking gate. Whoever recognises it has recognised it, and whoever is ignorant of it is ignorant of it. That is the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, and us.'

Whoever recognises it has recognised it, and whoever denies it has denied it. That is the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, and us.'

2. Muhammad B. Isa al-Ubaydi, raising it, narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} said:

'Allah has refused to let things proceed except through their means. So He appointed for everything a means, and for every means an explanation, and for every explanation a key, and for every key a knowledge, and for every knowledge a speaking gate. Whoever recognises it has recognised it, and whoever denies it has denied it. That is the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, and us.'

manual_chain: Ali B. Muhammad al-Qasani, from Muhammad B. Isa al-Ubaydi, raising it, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.

So He appointed for everything a means, and for every means an explanation, and for every explanation a key, and for every key a knowledge, and for every knowledge a speaking gate. Whoever recognises it has recognised it, and whoever denies it has denied it. That is the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, and us.'

chapter: Chapter 3: Recognition of the Scholar — whoever recognises him recognises Allah, and whoever denies him denies Allah the Exalted; and the cause by which one is granted recognition of him
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Abdullah B. Ja'far, from Muhammad B. Isa, from al-Husayn B. al-Munzir, from Umar B. Qays al-Masir, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Indeed, Allah did not leave anything that the Ummah would need up to the Day of Resurrection but that He revealed it in His Book and made it clear to His Messenger; and He set for everything a limit, and set over it a guide that points to it.'
englishText:
3. Umar B. Qays al-Masir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Indeed, Allah did not leave anything that the Ummah would need up to the Day of Resurrection but that He revealed it in His Book and made it clear to His Messenger; and He set for everything a limit, and set over it a guide that points to it.'
arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الْمُنْذِرِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ الْمَاصِرِ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَمْ يَدَعْ شَيْئاً يَحْتَاجُ إِلَيْهِ الْأُمَّةُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ إِلَّا أَنْزَلَهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ وَ بَيَّنَهُ لِرَسُولِهِ وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَدّاً وَ جَعَلَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًا يَدُلُّ عَلَيْهِ.
manual_chain: Abdullah B. Ja'far, from Muhammad B. Isa, from al-Husayn B. al-Munzir, from Umar B. Qays al-Masir, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Umar B. Qays al-Masir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Indeed, Allah did not leave anything that the Ummah would need up to the Day of Resurrection but that He revealed it in His Book and made it clear to His Messenger; and He set for everything a limit, and set over it a guide that points to it.'

chapter: Chapter 3: Recognition of the Scholar — whoever recognises him recognises Allah, and whoever denies him denies Allah the Exalted; and the cause by which one is granted recognition of him
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibrahim B. Hashim, from Yahya B. Abi Imran, from Yunus, from al-Husayn B. Munzir, from Umar B. Qays, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: (The same report as Hadith 3.)
englishText: 4. Umar B. Qays narrates the same report (Hadith 3) from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
arabicText: 4- وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُنْذِرٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ.

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Yaqoub B. Yazeed and Ibrahim B. Hashim, from Ibn Abi Umeyr, from Sayf B. Ameyra, from Abu Hamza al-Thumali, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'A scholar by whose knowledge people benefit is better than the worship of seventy thousand worshippers.'
englishText:
1. Abu Hamza al-Thumali narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'A scholar by whose knowledge people benefit is better than the worship of seventy thousand worshippers.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ وَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةَ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثُّمَالِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: عَالِمٌ يُنْتَفَعُ بِعِلْمِهِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ عِبَادَةِ سَبْعِينَ أَلْفَ عَابِدٍ.
manual_chain: Yaqoub B. Yazeed and Ibrahim B. Hashim, from Ibn Abi Umeyr, from Sayf B. Ameyra, from Abu Hamza al-Thumali, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abu Hamza al-Thumali narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'A scholar by whose knowledge people benefit is better than the worship of seventy thousand worshippers.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Hammad B. Isa, from Abdullah B. Maymoun, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon.'
englishText:
2. Abdullah B. Maymoun narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, who said: Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'The merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص فَضْلُ الْعَالِمِ عَلَى الْعَابِدِ كَفَضْلِ الْقَمَرِ عَلَى سَائِرِ النُّجُومِ لَيْلَةَ الْبَدْرِ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Hammad B. Isa, from Abdullah B. Maymoun, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abdullah B. Maymoun narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, who said: Rasulullah {{SAW}} said:

'The merit of the scholar over the worshipper is like the merit of the moon over the rest of the stars on the night of the full moon.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: And from him, by the same chain as Hadith 2: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Hammad B. Isa, from Abdullah B. Maymoun, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The merit of the scholar is more beloved to me than the merit of worship.'
englishText:
3. Abdullah B. Maymoun narrates, by the same chain (Hadith 2), that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, said:

'The merit of the scholar is more beloved to me than the merit of worship.'
arabicText: 3- وَ عَنْهُ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ: فَضْلُ الْعَالِمِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ فَضْلِ الْعِبَادَةِ.
manual_chain: And from him, by the same chain as Hadith 2: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from Hammad B. Isa, from Abdullah B. Maymoun, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abdullah B. Maymoun narrates, by the same chain (Hadith 2), that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, said:

'The merit of the scholar is more beloved to me than the merit of worship.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. Hassan and Zayd, from al-Rawandi, from Ja'far B. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The possessor of knowledge will arrive ahead of the worshipper by a journey of five hundred years.'
englishText:
4. al-Rawandi narrates that Ja'far B. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'The possessor of knowledge will arrive ahead of the worshipper by a journey of five hundred years.'
arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ وَ زَيْدٌ عَنِ الرَّاوَنْدِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ع قَالَ: يَأْتِي صَاحِبُ الْعِلْمِ قُدَّامَ الْعَابِدِ بِرَوْحَةٍ مَسِيرَةَ خَمْسِ مِائَةِ عَامٍ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. Hassan and Zayd, from al-Rawandi, from Ja'far B. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
al-Rawandi narrates that Ja'far B. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'The possessor of knowledge will arrive ahead of the worshipper by a journey of five hundred years.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Yaqoub B. Yazeed, from Ibn Abi Umeyr, from Sayf B. Ameyra, from Abu Hamza al-Thumali, from Ali B. al-Husayn (al-Sajjad) {{AS}}, or from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'A man of deep understanding in the religion is harder on Satan than the worship of a thousand worshippers.'
englishText:
5. Abu Hamza al-Thumali narrates that Ali B. al-Husayn (al-Sajjad) {{AS}} — or Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} — is reported to have said:

'A man of deep understanding in the religion is harder on Satan than the worship of a thousand worshippers.'
arabicText: 5- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ سَيْفِ بْنِ عَمِيرَةَ عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ الثُّمَالِيِّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ أَوْ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مُتَفَقِّهٌ فِي الدِّينِ أَشَدُّ عَلَى الشَّيْطَانِ مِنْ عِبَادَةِ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ.
manual_chain: Yaqoub B. Yazeed, from Ibn Abi Umeyr, from Sayf B. Ameyra, from Abu Hamza al-Thumali, from Ali B. al-Husayn (al-Sajjad) {{AS}}, or from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Abu Hamza al-Thumali narrates that Ali B. al-Husayn (al-Sajjad) {{AS}} — or Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} — is reported to have said:

'A man of deep understanding in the religion is harder on Satan than the worship of a thousand worshippers.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Muhammad B. Ismail, from Sa'dan B. Muslim, from Muawiya B. Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The narrator of our hadith, spreading it among the people and fixing it firmly in the hearts of our Shia, is superior to a thousand worshippers.'
englishText:
6. Muawiya B. Ammar narrates: I said to Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}: 'A man who is a narrator of your hadith, spreading it among the people and fixing it firmly in the hearts of your Shia — and perhaps there is a worshipper among your Shia who does not have this narration: which of the two is superior?'

He {{AS}} said: 'The narrator of our hadith, spreading it among the people and fixing it firmly in the hearts of our Shia, is superior to a thousand worshippers.'
arabicText: 6- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ سَعْدَانَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع رَجُلٌ رَاوِيَةٌ لِحَدِيثِكُمْ يَبُثُّ ذَلِكَ إِلَى النَّاسِ وَ يُسَدِّدُهُ فِي قُلُوبِ شِيعَتِكُمْ وَ لَعَلَّ عَابِداً مِنْ شِيعَتِكُمْ لَيْسَتْ لَهُ هَذِهِ الرِّوَايَةُ أَيُّهُمَا أَفْضَلُ قَالَ رَاوِيَةُ حَدِيثِنَا يَبُثُّ فِي النَّاسِ وَ يُسَدِّدُهُ فِي قُلُوبِ شِيعَتِنَا أَفْضَلُ مِنْ أَلْفِ عَابِدٍ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from Muhammad B. Ismail, from Sa'dan B. Muslim, from Muawiya B. Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Muawiya B. Ammar narrates: I said to Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}: 'A man who is a narrator of your hadith, spreading it among the people and fixing it firmly in the hearts of your Shia — and perhaps there is a worshipper among your Shia who does not have this narration: which of the two is superior?'

He {{AS}} said: 'The narrator of our hadith, spreading it among the people and fixing it firmly in the hearts of our Shia, is superior to a thousand worshippers.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. Isa B. Yunus B. Abdul Rahman, from the one who reported it, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'When it is the Day of Resurrection, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will raise the scholar and the worshipper. When the two of them stand before Allah, He will say to the worshipper: "Go on to Paradise!" And it will be said to the scholar: "Stay, and intercede for the people for the excellence with which you taught them."'
englishText:
7. Muhammad B. Isa B. Yunus B. Abdul Rahman, from the one who reported it, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who said:

'When it is the Day of Resurrection, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will raise the scholar and the worshipper. When the two of them stand before Allah, He will say to the worshipper: "Go on to Paradise!" And it will be said to the scholar: "Stay, and intercede for the people for the excellence with which you taught them."'
arabicText: 7- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى بْنِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَمَّنْ رَوَاهُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْقِيَامَةِ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ الْعَالِمَ وَ الْعَابِدَ فَإِذَا وَقَفَا بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ قَالَ لِلْعَابِدِ انْطَلِقْ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ قِيلَ لِلْعَالِمِ قِفْ تَشْفَعْ لِلنَّاسِ بِحُسْنِ تَأْدِيبِكَ لَهُمْ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. Isa B. Yunus B. Abdul Rahman, from the one who reported it, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Muhammad B. Isa B. Yunus B. Abdul Rahman, from the one who reported it, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who said:

'When it is the Day of Resurrection, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will raise the scholar and the worshipper. When the two of them stand before Allah, He will say to the worshipper: "Go on to Paradise!" And it will be said to the scholar: "Stay, and intercede for the people for the excellence with which you taught them."'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper

thaqalaynMatn: 'The superiority of the scholar over the worshipper is like the superiority of the sun over the stars; and the superiority of the worshipper over the one who does not worship is like the superiority of the moon over the stars.'

'The superiority of the scholar over the worshipper is like the superiority of the sun over the stars; and the superiority of the worshipper over the one who does not worship is like the superiority of the moon over the stars.'

chapter: Chapter 4: Merits of the Scholar over the Worshipper
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Mahboub, from Muawiya B. Wahb, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'The narrator of hadith, the one with deep understanding in the religion, is better than a thousand worshippers who have neither understanding nor narration.'
englishText:
10. Muawiya B. Wahb narrates: I asked Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} about two men — one of them a jurist who narrates hadith, and the other a worshipper who has nothing like his narrations.

He {{AS}} said: 'The narrator of hadith, the one with deep understanding in the religion, is better than a thousand worshippers who have neither understanding nor narration.'

chapter: Chapter 6: The People Are Commanded to Seek Knowledge from Its Source — and Its Source Is the Progeny of Muhammad
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: al-Sindi B. Muhammad, from Aban B. Usman, from Abdullah B. Suleyman, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Then the believer of the people of Pharaoh is destroyed! Knowledge has never ceased to be concealed since the day Allah sent Noah. Let al-Hasan go right and left — by Allah, he will not find the knowledge except right here.'
englishText:
1. Abdullah B. Suleyman narrates that he heard Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} — while a man from the people of Basra called Uthman the Blind was with him, claiming that al-Hasan al-Basri said those who conceal knowledge are harmed by the stench of their own bellies among the inhabitants of the Fire — respond:

'Then the believer of the people of Pharaoh is destroyed! Knowledge has never ceased to be concealed since the day Allah sent Noah. Let al-Hasan go right and left — by Allah, he will not find the knowledge except right here.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي السِّنْدِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع وَ عِنْدَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْبَصْرَةِ يُقَالُ لَهُ عُثْمَانُ الْأَعْمَى وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْحَسَنَ الْبَصْرِيَّ يَزْعُمُ أَنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ الْعِلْمَ يُؤْذِي رِيحُ بُطُونِهِمْ أَهْلَ النَّارِ فَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع إِذاً فَهَلَكَ مُؤْمِنُ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ وَ مَا زَالَ الْعِلْمُ مَكْتُوماً مُنْذُ بَعَثَ اللَّهُ نُوحاً ع فَلْيَذْهَبِ الْحَسَنُ يَمِيناً وَ شِمَالًا فَوَ اللَّهِ مَا يُوجَدُ الْعِلْمُ إِلَّا هَاهُنَا.

chapter: Chapter 6 (Rare Narrations): The Scholars Are the Progeny of Muhammad

arabicText: 3- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ رَفَعَهُ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ إِنَّ الْعُلَمَاءَ وَرَثَةُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَ ذَلِكَ أَنَّ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ لَمْ يُوَرِّثُوا دِينَاراً وَ لَا دِرْهَماً وَ إِنَّمَا وَرَّثُوا أَحَادِيثَ مِنْ أَحَادِيثِهِمْ فَمَنْ أَخَذَ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْهَا فَقَدْ أَخَذَ حَظّاً وَافِراً فَانْظُرُوا عِلْمَكُمْ هَذَا عَمَّنْ تَأْخُذُونَهُ فَإِنَّ فِينَا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فِي كُلِّ خَلَفٍ عُدُولٌ يَنْفُونَ عَنْهُ تَحْرِيفَ الْغَالِينَ وَ انْتِحَالَ الْمُبْطِلِينَ وَ تَأْوِيلَ الْجَاهِلِينَ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Hasan B. Ali B. Fazzal — raised to Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
al-Hasan B. Ali B. Fazzal raises it to Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, who is reported to have said:

'The scholars are the heirs of the Prophets, for the Prophets leave behind no dinars or gold as inheritance — rather they leave behind their sayings. So whoever takes anything from them has taken an abundant share. Therefore look at this knowledge of yours — who have you taken it from? For among us, in every generation there is one who restores the balance, negating from it the distortions of the extremists, the plagiarism of the falsifiers, and the interpretations of the ignorant.'

arabicText: 4- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْبَرْقِيِّ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ الْأَزْدِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ الْعَبْدِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ أَبِيهِ ع عَنْ عَلِيٍّ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص قِرَاءَةُ الْقُرْآنِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ قِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ فِي غَيْرِ الصَّلَاةِ وَ ذِكْرُ اللَّهِ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ الصَّدَقَةِ وَ الصَّدَقَةُ أَفْضَلُ مِنَ الصَّوْمِ وَ الصَّوْمُ جُنَّةٌ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لَا قَوْلَ إِلَّا بِعَمَلٍ وَ لَا عَمَلَ إِلَّا بِنِيَّةٍ وَ لَا نِيَّةَ إِلَّا بِإِصَابَةِ السُّنَّةِ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Barqi, from Ibrahim B. Is'haq al-Azdi, from Abu Usman al-Abdi, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father, Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, from Ameer al-Mu'minin (Imam Ali) {{AS}}.

chapter: Chapter 7: Regarding the Imams of the Progeny of Muhammad — The Knowledge Is to Be Drawn from Them, and They Are Scholars Who Are Neither Unjust nor Ignorant
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibrahim B. Is'haq, from Abdullah B. Hammad, from Sabbah al-Muzani, from al-Harith B. Hasira, from al-Hakam B. Utayba.

manual_chain: Ibrahim B. Is'haq, from Abdullah B. Hammad, from Sabbah al-Muzani, from al-Harith B. Hasira, from al-Hakam B. Utayba.

chapter: Chapter 7: Regarding the Imams of the Progeny of Muhammad — The Knowledge Is to Be Drawn from Them, and They Are Scholars Who Are Neither Unjust nor Ignorant
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: al-Haytham al-Nahdi al-Kufi, from al-Hasan B. Ali, from Ibn Harasa al-Shaybani, from a shaykh from the people of Kufa.
thaqalaynMatn: 'I saw Ali b. al-Husayn (al-Sajjad) {{AS}} at Mina. arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا الْهَيْثَمُ النَّهْدِيُّ الْكُوفِيُّ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنِ ابْنِ هَرَاسَةَ الشَّيْبَانِيِّ عَنْ شَيْخٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ قَالَ رَأَيْتُ عَلِيَّ بْنَ الْحُسَيْنِ ع بِمِنًى فَقَالَ مِمَّنِ الرَّجُلُ فَقُلْتُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ فَقَالَ لِي يَا أَخَا أَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ أَمَا لَوْ كُنْتَ عِنْدَنَا بِالْمَدِينَةِ لَأَرَيْنَاكَ مَوَاطِنَ جَبْرَئِيلَ مِنْ دُوَيْرِنَا اسْتَقَانَا النَّاسُ الْعِلْمَ فَتَرَاهُمْ عَلِمُوا وَ جَهِلْنَا.

chapter: Chapter 7: Regarding the Imams of the Progeny of Muhammad — The Knowledge Is to Be Drawn from Them, and They Are Scholars Who Are Neither Unjust nor Ignorant
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Hasan B. Mahboub, from Yahya B. Abdullah Abu al-Hasan, sahib al-Daylam, from Ja'far B. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'How strange the people are! They have taken all of their knowledge from the Messenger of Allah {{SAW}}, then acted on it and were guided by it — yet they regard us, the People of his {{SAW}} House and his offspring, as not having taken his knowledge, while we are the People of his House and his descendants, in whose homes the Revelation came down, and from whom the knowledge went out to them. Do they think they came to know and were guided, while we are ignorant and have strayed? Surely this is impossible!'

chapter: Chapter 7 (Rare Narrations)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. al-Jufi, from Ja'far B. Bashir and al-Hasan B. Ali B. Fazzal, from Muthanna, from Zurara.
thaqalaynMatn: I was sitting with Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} when a man from the people of Kufa asked him {{AS}} about the saying of Ameer al-Mu'mineen (Imam Ali) {{AS}}: “Ask me about whatever you wish, and you will not ask me about anything without my informing you of it.” He {{AS}} said: “There is no one who possesses any knowledge except that it came from Ameer al-Mu'mineen {{AS}}. So let the people go wherever they wish — by Allah, the matter will come to them from right here” — and he {{AS}} gestured with his hand toward Medina.
englishText:
1. Zurara narrates:

I was sitting with Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} when a man from the people of Kufa asked him {{AS}} about the saying of Ameer al-Mu'mineen (Imam Ali) {{AS}}: “Ask me about whatever you wish, and you will not ask me about anything without my informing you of it.” He {{AS}} said: “There is no one who possesses any knowledge except that it came from Ameer al-Mu'mineen {{AS}}. So let the people go wherever they wish — by Allah, the matter will come to them from right here” — and he {{AS}} gestured with his hand toward Medina.
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْجُعْفِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ مُثَنَّى عَنْ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ كُنْتُ قَاعِداً عِنْدَ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ يَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ قَوْلِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع سَلُونِي عَمَّا شِئْتُمْ وَ لَا تَسْأَلُونِي عَنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا أَنْبَأْتُكُمْ بِهِ فَقَالَ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ عِنْدَهُ أَحَدٌ عِلْمٌ إِلَّا خَرَجَ مِنْ عِنْدِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع فَلْيَذْهَبِ النَّاسُ حَيْثُ شَاءُوا فَوَ اللَّهِ لَيَأْتِيهِمُ الْأَمْرُ مِنْ هَاهُنَا وَ أَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. al-Jufi, from Ja'far B. Bashir and al-Hasan B. Ali B. Fazzal, from Muthanna, from Zurara.
manual_body:
Zurara narrates:

I was sitting with Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} when a man from the people of Kufa asked him {{AS}} about the saying of Ameer al-Mu'mineen (Imam Ali) {{AS}}: “Ask me about whatever you wish, and you will not ask me about anything without my informing you of it.” He {{AS}} said: “There is no one who possesses any knowledge except that it came from Ameer al-Mu'mineen {{AS}}. So let the people go wherever they wish — by Allah, the matter will come to them from right here” — and he {{AS}} gestured with his hand toward Medina.

chapter: Chapter 8: Regarding the Strayers — Those Who Strayed from the Imams of Truth and Took the Religion by Opinion, Without Guidance from the Imams of Truth
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from al-Nadr B. Suwayd, from al-Qasim B. Suleyman, from al-Mu'alla B. Khunays, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'It means one who takes his own opinion as his religion, without guidance from the Imams of guidance.'
englishText:
1. al-Mu'alla B. Khunays narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, regarding the words of Allah, Mighty and Majestic — “And who is more astray than one who follows his own whim without guidance from Allah?” [28:50] — said:

'It means one who takes his own opinion as his religion, without guidance from the Imams of guidance.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ عَنِ النَّضْرِ بْنِ سُوَيْدٍ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ عَنِ الْمُعَلَّى بْنِ خُنَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فِي قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّنِ اتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنَ اللَّهِ يَعْنِي مَنْ يَتَّخِذُ دِينَهُ رَأْيَهُ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ الْهُدَى.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Muhammad, from al-Husayn B. Saeed, from al-Nadr B. Suwayd, from al-Qasim B. Suleyman, from al-Mu'alla B. Khunays, from Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
al-Mu'alla B. Khunays narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, regarding the words of Allah, Mighty and Majestic — “And who is more astray than one who follows his own whim without guidance from Allah?” [28:50] — said:

'It means one who takes his own opinion as his religion, without guidance from the Imams of guidance.'

chapter: Chapter 8 (Rare Narrations)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Yaqoub B. Yazeed, from Is'haq B. Ammar, from Ahmad B. al-Nadr, from Amro B. Shimr, from Jabir B. Yazeed, from Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Whoever takes a religion before Allah without hearing it from a truthful one, Allah binds it upon him irrevocably, until the Day of Resurrection.'
englishText:
1. Jabir narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} is reported to have said:

'Whoever takes a religion before Allah without hearing it from a truthful one, Allah binds it upon him irrevocably, until the Day of Resurrection.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ النَّضْرِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ ع أَنَّهُ قَالَ مَنْ دَانَ اللَّهَ بِغَيْرِ سَمَاعٍ عَنْ صَادِقٍ أَلْزَمَهُ اللَّهُ الْبَتَّةَ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ.

chapter: Chapter 8 (Rare Narrations)
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: al-Husayn B. Muhammad, from Mu'alla B. Muhammad, from Ahmad B. Muhammad al-Sayyari, from Ali B. Abdullah.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Whoever affirms the Imams, follows their command, and does not overstep their obedience.'
englishText:
2. Ali B. Abdullah narrates: a man asked him {{AS}} about the words of Allah, Mighty and Majestic — “Whoever follows My guidance shall not go astray nor suffer” [20:123]. He {{AS}} said:

'Whoever affirms the Imams, follows their command, and does not overstep their obedience.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُعَلَّى بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ السَّيَّارِيِّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ سَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَمَنِ اتَّبَعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَ لَا يَشْقَى قَالَ مَنْ قَالَ بِالْأَئِمَّةِ وَ اتَّبَعَ أَمْرَهُمْ وَ لَمْ يَجُزْ طَاعَتَهُمْ.
manual_chain: al-Husayn B. Muhammad, from Mu'alla B. Muhammad, from Ahmad B. Muhammad al-Sayyari, from Ali B. Abdullah.
manual_body:
Ali B. Abdullah narrates: a man asked him {{AS}} about the words of Allah, Mighty and Majestic — “Whoever follows My guidance shall not go astray nor suffer” [20:123]. He {{AS}} said:

'Whoever affirms the Imams, follows their command, and does not overstep their obedience.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibn Isa, from Ibn Mahboub, from Bishr B. [Abi Uqba], [from] Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) and Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} from a clay, from an essence beneath the Throne, and his clay had a maturing. So the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}} was moulded from the matured clay of Rasulullah {{SAW}}, and the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}} had a maturing. So our clay was moulded from the surplus of the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}}, and our clay had a maturing. So the clay of our Shia was moulded from the matured of our clay. Thus their hearts yearn towards us and our hearts are tender towards them, tenderer than a father over his child; and we are better for them and they are better for us, and Rasulullah {{SAW}} is better for us and we are better for him.'
englishText:
1. Bishr B. Abi Uqba narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) and Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} said:

'Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} from a clay, from an essence beneath the Throne, and his clay had a maturing. So the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}} was moulded from the matured clay of Rasulullah {{SAW}}, and the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}} had a maturing. So our clay was moulded from the surplus of the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}}, and our clay had a maturing. So the clay of our Shia was moulded from the matured of our clay. Thus their hearts yearn towards us and our hearts are tender towards them, tenderer than a father over his child; and we are better for them and they are better for us, and Rasulullah {{SAW}} is better for us and we are better for him.'
arabicText: 1- حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عِيسَى عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ بِشْرِ بْنِ [أَبِي عُقْبَةَ] [عَنْ] أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً ص مِنْ طِينَةٍ مِنْ جَوْهَرَةٍ تَحْتَ الْعَرْشِ وَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ لِطِينَتِهِ نَضْجٌ فَجَبَلَ طِينَةَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع مِنْ نَضْجِ طِينَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص وَ كَانَ لِطِينَةِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع نَضْجٌ فَجَبَلَ طِينَتَنَا مِنْ فَضْلِ طِينَةِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ع وَ كَانَتْ لِطِينَتِنَا نَضْجٌ فَجَبَلَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ نَضْجِ طِينَتِنَا فَقُلُوبُهُمْ تَحِنُّ إِلَيْنَا وَ قُلُوبُنَا تَعْطِفُ عَلَيْهِمْ تَعَطُّفَ الْوَالِدِ عَلَى الْوَلَدِ وَ نَحْنُ خَيْرٌ لَهُمْ وَ هُمْ خَيْرٌ لَنَا وَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ لَنَا خَيْرٌ وَ نَحْنُ لَهُ خَيْرٌ.
manual_chain: Ibn Isa, from Ibn Mahboub, from Bishr B. [Abi Uqba], [from] Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) and Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}.
manual_body:
Bishr B. Abi Uqba narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) and Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} said:

'Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} from a clay, from an essence beneath the Throne, and his clay had a maturing. So the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}} was moulded from the matured clay of Rasulullah {{SAW}}, and the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}} had a maturing. So our clay was moulded from the surplus of the clay of Amir al-Mu'mineen {{AS}}, and our clay had a maturing. So the clay of our Shia was moulded from the matured of our clay. Thus their hearts yearn towards us and our hearts are tender towards them, tenderer than a father over his child; and we are better for them and they are better for us, and Rasulullah {{SAW}} is better for us and we are better for him.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. Isa, from Abu al-Hajjaj.
thaqalaynMatn: 'O Abu al-Hajjaj! Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} and the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}} from the clay of Illiyyin, and created their hearts from a clay above that; and He created our Shia from a clay below Illiyyin, and created their hearts from the clay of Illiyyin — so the hearts of our Shia are from the bodies of the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}}. And Allah created the enemy of the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}} from the clay of Sijjin, and created their hearts from a clay fouler than that; and He created their followers from a clay below the clay of Sijjin, and created their hearts from the clay of Sijjin — so their hearts are from the bodies of those ones. And every heart yearns towards its own body.'
englishText:
2. Abu al-Hajjaj narrates that Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} said to him:

'O Abu al-Hajjaj! Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} and the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}} from the clay of Illiyyin, and created their hearts from a clay above that; and He created our Shia from a clay below Illiyyin, and created their hearts from the clay of Illiyyin — so the hearts of our Shia are from the bodies of the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}}. And Allah created the enemy of the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}} from the clay of Sijjin, and created their hearts from a clay fouler than that; and He created their followers from a clay below the clay of Sijjin, and created their hearts from the clay of Sijjin — so their hearts are from the bodies of those ones. And every heart yearns towards its own body.'
arabicText: 2- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ أَبِي الْحَجَّاجِ قَالَ قَالَ لِي أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع يَا أَبَا الْحَجَّاجِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً وَ آلَ مُحَمَّدٍ ص مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةٍ فَوْقِ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ شِيعَتَنَا مِنْ طِينَةٍ دُونَ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَةِ عِلِّيِّينَ فَقُلُوبُ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ أَبْدَانِ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ عَدُوَّ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ ص مِنْ طِينِ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ أَخْبَثَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَ خَلَقَ شِيعَتَهُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ دُونَ طِينِ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ قُلُوبَهُمْ مِنْ طِينِ سِجِّينٍ فَقُلُوبُهُمْ مِنْ أَبْدَانِ أُولَئِكَ وَ كُلُّ قَلْبٍ يَحِنُّ إِلَى بَدَنِهِ.

And Allah created the enemy of the Progeny of Muhammad {{SAW}} from the clay of Sijjin, and created their hearts from a clay fouler than that; and He created their followers from a clay below the clay of Sijjin, and created their hearts from the clay of Sijjin — so their hearts are from the bodies of those ones. And every heart yearns towards its own body.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ibn Isa, from Muhammad al-Barqi, from Abu Nahshal, from Muhammad B. Ismail, from al-Thumali (Abu Hamza).
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah created us from the highest Illiyyin, and created the hearts of our Shia from that which He created us from, and created their bodies from below that — so their hearts incline towards us because they were created from what we were created from.' Then he {{AS}} recited this verse: “Never! Indeed the record of the righteous is in Illiyyin. And what will make you realise what Illiyyin is? A written record, witnessed by those brought near.” [83:18-21] 'And He created our enemy from Sijjin, and created the hearts of their Shia from that which He created them from, and their bodies from below that — so their hearts incline towards them because they were created from what they were created from.' Then he {{AS}} recited this verse: “Never! Indeed the record of the wicked is in Sijjin. And what will make you realise what Sijjin is? A written record.” [83:7-9]
englishText:
3. Abu Hamza al-Thumali narrates: I heard Abu Ja'far (al-Baqir) {{AS}} saying:

'Allah created us from the highest Illiyyin, and created the hearts of our Shia from that which He created us from, and created their bodies from below that — so their hearts incline towards us because they were created from what we were created from.' Then he {{AS}} recited this verse: “Never! Indeed the record of the righteous is in Illiyyin. And what will make you realise what Illiyyin is? A written record, witnessed by those brought near.” [83:18-21] 'And He created our enemy from Sijjin, and created the hearts of their Shia from that which He created them from, and their bodies from below that — so their hearts incline towards them because they were created from what they were created from.' Then he {{AS}} recited this verse: “Never! Indeed the record of the wicked is in Sijjin. And what will make you realise what Sijjin is? A written record.” [83:7-9]

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. al-Husayn, from Ahmad B. Ali B. Haytham, from Idris, from Muhammad B. Sinan al-Abdi, from Jabir al-Ju'fi.
thaqalaynMatn: 'O Jabir! We and those who love us were created from one clay — pure and white, from the highest Illiyyin. We were created from its uppermost part, and those who love us were created from below it. So when the Day of Resurrection comes, the upper will fold together with the lower. And when the Day of Resurrection comes, we shall lay our hands upon the waist-sash (ḥujza) of our Prophet {{SAW}}, and our Shia will lay their hands upon our waist-sash. So where do you think Allah will place His Prophet {{SAW}} and his offspring, and where do you think his offspring will place those who love them?' Then Jabir struck his hand upon his (the Imam's) hand and said: 'We have surely entered it — by the Lord of the Ka'ba!' — three times.

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. al-Husayn, from al-Nadr B. Shuayb, from Abdul Ghaffar al-Jazi.
thaqalaynMatn:
'Allah created the believer from the clay of Paradise, and created the Nasibi (the hostile one) from the clay of the Fire.'

And he {{AS}} said: 'When Allah desires good for a servant, He makes his soul and body wholesome, so that he hears nothing good without recognising it, and hears nothing evil without rejecting it.'

[Abdul Ghaffar] said: And I heard him {{AS}} saying: 'The clays are three: the clay of the Prophets — and the believer is from that same clay, except that the Prophets are its purest essence and are the origin, and to them belongs their pre-eminence; and the believers are the branch, from sticky clay. Even so, Allah makes no separation between them and their Shia.'

And he {{AS}} said: 'The clay of the Nasibi is from putrid black mud; and as for the oppressed ones (al-mustaḍʿafun), they are from dust. No believer turns away from his faith, nor a Nasibi from his hostility; and to Allah belongs the will concerning them all.'

'Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created us from Illiyyin, and created those who love us from below that which He created us from; and He created our enemy from Sijjin, and created those who love them from that which He created them from. So for that reason, each inclines towards its like.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Imran B. Musa, from Musa B. Ja'far, from Ali B. Ma'bad, from Ibrahim B. Is'haq, from al-Husayn B. Yazeed, from Ja'far B. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, from his grandfather {{AS}}.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah sent Jibraeel to Paradise, and he brought Him clay from its clay; and He sent the Angel of Death to the earth, and he brought Him clay from its clay. arabicText: 10- حَدَّثَنِي عِمْرَانُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ عَنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ع إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَعَثَ جَبْرَئِيلَ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ فَأَتَاهُ بِطِينَةٍ مِنْ طِينَتِهَا وَ بَعَثَ مَلَكَ الْمَوْتِ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ فَجَاءَهُ بِطِينَةٍ مِنْ طِينَتِهَا فَجَمَعَ الطِّينَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ قَسَمَهَا نِصْفَيْنِ فَجَعَلَنَا مِنْ خَيْرِ الْقِسْمَيْنِ وَ جَعَلَ شِيعَتَنَا مِنْ طِينَتِنَا فَمَا كَانَ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا مِمَّا يُرْغَبُ بِهِمْ عَنْهُ مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ الْقَبِيحَةِ فَذَاكَ لِمَا خَالَطَهُمْ مِنَ الطِّينَةِ الْخَبِيثَةِ وَ مَصِيرُهَا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَ مَا كَانَ فِي عَدُوِّنَا مِنْ بِرٍّ وَ صَلَاةٍ وَ صَوْمٍ وَ مِنَ الْأَعْمَالِ الْحَسَنَةِ فَذَاكَ لِمَا خَالَطَهُمْ مِنْ طِينَتِنَا الطَّيِّبَةِ وَ مَصِيرُهُمْ إِلَى النَّارِ.

al-Husayn B. Yazeed narrates from Ja'far b. Muhammad (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, from his father (al-Baqir) {{AS}}, from his grandfather {{AS}}, who said: Ali b. al-Husayn (al-Sajjad) {{AS}} said:

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Muhammad B. Hammad, from his brother Ahmad B. Hammad, from Ibrahim B. Abdul Hameed, from his father.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah created the Prophets and the successors on the Day of Friday, and it is the day on which Allah took their covenant.' And he {{AS}} said: 'We and our Shia were created from a treasured clay (ṭīna makhzuna), from which none shall stray — not a single one — until the Day of Resurrection.'
englishText:
11. Ibrahim B. Abdul Hameed, from his father, who said: I heard Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (al-Kadhim) {{AS}} saying:

'Allah created the Prophets and the successors on the Day of Friday, and it is the day on which Allah took their covenant.' And he {{AS}} said: 'We and our Shia were created from a treasured clay (ṭīna makhzuna), from which none shall stray — not a single one — until the Day of Resurrection.'
arabicText: 11- حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ أَخِيهِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْحَمِيدِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ الْأَنْبِيَاءَ وَ الْأَوْصِيَاءَ يَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ وَ هُوَ الْيَوْمُ الَّذِي أَخَذَ اللَّهُ فِيهِ مِيثَاقَهُمْ وَ قَالَ خُلِقْنَا نَحْنُ وَ شِيعَتُنَا مِنْ طِينَةٍ مَخْزُونَةٍ لَا يَشِذُّ مِنْهَا شَاذٌّ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ.
manual_chain: Muhammad B. Hammad, from his brother Ahmad B. Hammad, from Ibrahim B. Abdul Hameed, from his father.
manual_body:
Ibrahim B. Abdul Hameed, from his father, who said: I heard Abu al-Hasan al-Awwal (al-Kadhim) {{AS}} saying:

'Allah created the Prophets and the successors on the Day of Friday, and it is the day on which Allah took their covenant.' And he {{AS}} said: 'We and our Shia were created from a treasured clay (ṭīna makhzuna), from which none shall stray — not a single one — until the Day of Resurrection.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge

thaqalaynSanad: Ahmad B. Musa, from al-Hasan B. Musa, from Ali B. Hassan, from Abdul Rahman B. Kaseer.
thaqalaynMatn: 'Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} and his household (ʿitra) from the clay of the Throne; so not one of them is diminished, nor is one of them added.'
englishText:
12. Abdul Rahman B. Kaseer narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} said:

'Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} and his household (ʿitra) from the clay of the Throne; so not one of them is diminished, nor is one of them added.'
arabicText: 12- حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُوسَى عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ مُحَمَّداً وَ عِتْرَتَهُ مِنْ طِينَةِ الْعَرْشِ فَلَا يَنْقُصُ مِنْهُمْ وَاحِدٌ وَ لَا يَزِيدُ مِنْهُمْ وَاحِدٌ.
manual_chain: Ahmad B. Musa, from al-Hasan B. Musa, from Ali B. Hassan, from Abdul Rahman B. Kaseer.
manual_body:
Abdul Rahman B. Kaseer narrates that Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}} said:

'Allah created Muhammad {{SAW}} and his household (ʿitra) from the clay of the Throne; so not one of them is diminished, nor is one of them added.'

chapter: Chapter 9: In It Is the Creation of the Bodies of the Imams and Their Hearts, and the Bodies of the Shias and Their Hearts, Lest Exaggeration Enter the People Regarding the Wonders of Their Knowledge
author: Abū Jaʿfar Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Ṣaffār
thaqalaynSanad: Yaqoub B. Yazeed and Muhammad B. Isa, from Ziyad al-Qandi, from al-Fadl B. Isa al-Hashimi.
thaqalaynMatn: 'It is not as you are supposing. Allah created our clay from Illiyyin, and created the clay of our Shia from below that — so they are from us; and He created the clay of our enemy from Sijjin, and created the clay of their adherents from below that — and they are from them. And Salman is better than Luqman.'
englishText:
13. al-Fadl B. Isa al-Hashimi narrates: I entered upon Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, I and my father. My father said to him: 'Is it from the words of Rasulullah {{SAW}}: “Salman is a man from us, the People of the Household”?' He {{AS}} said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Meaning, from the sons of Abdul Muttalib?' He {{AS}} said: 'From us, the People of the Household.' He said to him: 'Meaning, from the sons of Abu Talib?' He {{AS}} said: 'From us, the People of the Household.' He said to him: 'I do not understand it.' He {{AS}} said: 'Then understand it, O Isa, for he is from us, the People of the Household.' Then he {{AS}} gestured with his hand towards his chest and said:

'It is not as you are supposing. Allah created our clay from Illiyyin, and created the clay of our Shia from below that — so they are from us; and He created the clay of our enemy from Sijjin, and created the clay of their adherents from below that — and they are from them. And Salman is better than Luqman.'
arabicText: 13- حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ زِيَادٍ الْقَنْدِيِّ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ عِيسَى الْهَاشِمِيِّ قَالَ دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَنَا وَ أَبِي فَقَالَ لَهُ أَ مِنْ قَوْلِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص سَلْمَانُ رَجُلٌ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ فَقَالَ أَيْ مِنْ وُلْدِ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ فَقَالَ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَيْ مِنْ وُلْدِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ فَقَالَ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ لَهُ إِنِّي لَا أَعْرِفُهُ فَقَالَ فَاعْرِفْهُ يَا عِيسَى فَإِنَّهُ مِنَّا أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ثُمَّ أَوْمَأَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى صَدْرِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَيْسَ حَيْثُ تَذْهَبُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ طِينَتَنَا مِنْ عِلِّيِّينَ وَ خَلَقَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِنَا مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ فَهُمْ مِنَّا وَ خَلَقَ طِينَةَ عَدُوِّنَا مِنْ سِجِّينٍ وَ خَلَقَ طِينَةَ شِيعَتِهِمْ مِنْ دُونِ ذَلِكَ وَ هُمْ مِنْهُمْ وَ سَلْمَانُ خَيْرٌ مِنْ لُقْمَانَ.
manual_chain: Yaqoub B. Yazeed and Muhammad B. Isa, from Ziyad al-Qandi, from al-Fadl B. Isa al-Hashimi.
manual_body:
al-Fadl B. Isa al-Hashimi narrates: I entered upon Abu Abdullah (al-Sadiq) {{AS}}, I and my father. My father said to him: 'Is it from the words of Rasulullah {{SAW}}: “Salman is a man from us, the People of the Household”?' He {{AS}} said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Meaning, from the sons of Abdul Muttalib?' He {{AS}} said: 'From us, the People of the Household.' He said to him: 'Meaning, from the sons of Abu Talib?' He {{AS}} said: 'From us, the People of the Household.' He said to him: 'I do not understand it.' He {{AS}} said: 'Then understand it, O Isa, for he is from us, the People of the Household.' Then he {{AS}} gestured with his hand towards his chest and said:

'It is not as you are supposing. Allah created our clay from Illiyyin, and created the clay of our Shia from below that — so they are from us; and He created the clay of our enemy from Sijjin, and created the clay of their adherents from below that — and they are from them. And Salman is better than Luqman.'
